title: Correct: stop and implicit keep semantics per RFC 5228 §4.5, §2.10.2
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT: eval_stmt returns StmtResult::Stop; eval_stmt_list short-circuits and returns Some(Stop); eval_script matches this in the wildcard arm and returns SieveAction::Keep (implicit keep). RFC 5228 §4.5 says stop terminates processing; §2.10.2 says implicit keep applies when no explicit disposition was produced. The code correctly handles all orderings: (1) stop alone → Keep; (2) fileinto before stop → FileInto (stops at FileInto, never reaches stop); (3) stop before fileinto → Keep (never reaches fileinto). The StmtResult::Keep variant from an explicit 'keep' command also propagates correctly via the wildcard arm.
